<content>
1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Progress Provider
3:  *
4:  * Provider-agnostic tree view data provider for the Cloud Agents sidebar.
5:  * Shows sessions with task details, external links, and status icons.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
9:  */
10: 
11: import {
12: 	EventEmitter,
13: 	ThemeIcon,
14: 	TreeItem,
15: 	TreeItemCollapsibleState,
16: 	Uri,
17: 	commands,
18: 	type Event,
19: 	type TreeDataProvider,
20: } from "vscode";
21: import type { AgentSessionStorage } from "../features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage";
22: import type { ProviderRegistry } from "../features/cloud-agents/provider-registry";
23: import type { AgentSession, AgentTask } from "../features/cloud-agents/types";
24: 
25: // ============================================================================
26: // Tree Item IDs
27: // ============================================================================
28: 
29: /**
30:  * Wrapper that carries session/task data through the tree.
31:  */
32: class CloudAgentTreeItem extends TreeItem {
33: 	readonly session: AgentSession | undefined;
34: 	readonly agentTask: AgentTask | undefined;
35: 
36: 	constructor(
37: 		label: string,
38: 		collapsibleState: TreeItemCollapsibleState,
39: 		session?: AgentSession,
40: 		agentTask?: AgentTask
41: 	) {
42: 		super(label, collapsibleState);
43: 		this.session = session;
44: 		this.agentTask = agentTask;
45: 	}
46: }
47: 
48: // ============================================================================
49: // CloudAgentProgressProvider
50: // ============================================================================
51: 
52: /**
53:  * Tree view data provider for the Cloud Agents sidebar.
54:  * Shows active provider sessions with tasks, external links, and PR info.
55:  *
56:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
57:  */
58: export class CloudAgentProgressProvider
59: 	implements TreeDataProvider<CloudAgentTreeItem>
60: {
61: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
62: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage | undefined;
63: 	private readonly _onDidChangeTreeData = new EventEmitter<
64: 		CloudAgentTreeItem | undefined | null
65: 	>();
66: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<CloudAgentTreeItem | undefined | null> =
67: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
68: 
69: 	constructor(
70: 		registry: ProviderRegistry,
71: 		sessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage
72: 	) {
73: 		this.registry = registry;
74: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	/**
78: 	 * Refresh the tree view.
79: 	 */
80: 	refresh(): void {
81: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(null);
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/**
85: 	 * Update the context key for welcome content visibility.
86: 	 */
87: 	async updateContextKeys(): Promise<void> {
88: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
89: 		await commands.executeCommand(
90: 			"setContext",
91: 			"gatomia.cloudAgent.hasProvider",
92: 			active !== undefined
93: 		);
94: 		if (active) {
95: 			await commands.executeCommand(
96: 				"setContext",
97: 				"gatomia.cloudAgent.activeProvider",
98: 				active.metadata.id
99: 			);
100: 		}
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	getTreeItem(element: CloudAgentTreeItem): CloudAgentTreeItem {
104: 		return element;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	async getChildren(
108: 		element?: CloudAgentTreeItem
109: 	): Promise<CloudAgentTreeItem[]> {
110: 		if (element?.session && !element.agentTask) {
111: 			return this.getSessionChildren(element.session);
112: 		}
113: 
114: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
115: 		if (!active) {
116: 			return [];
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		if (!this.sessionStorage) {
120: 			return [];
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		const allSessions = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
124: 		if (allSessions.length === 0) {
125: 			const item = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
126: 				"No sessions",
127: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
128: 			);
129: 			item.description = active.metadata.displayName;
130: 			return [item];
131: 		}
132: 
133: 		return allSessions.map((s) =>
134: 			this.createSessionItem(s, active.metadata.id)
135: 		);
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	private createSessionItem(
139: 		session: AgentSession,
140: 		activeProviderId: string
141: 	): CloudAgentTreeItem {
142: 		const firstTask = session.tasks[0];
143: 		const label = firstTask
144: 			? `${firstTask.specTaskId}: ${firstTask.title}`
145: 			: session.localId;
146: 
147: 		const hasTasks =
148: 			session.tasks.length > 0 || session.pullRequests.length > 0;
149: 		const item = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
150: 			label,
151: 			hasTasks
152: 				? TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed
153: 				: TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
154: 			session
155: 		);
156: 
157: 		item.description = session.isReadOnly
158: 			? `${session.status} (read-only)`
159: 			: session.status;
160: 
161: 		const tooltipLines = [
162: 			`Status: ${session.status}`,
163: 			`Provider: ${session.providerId}`,
164: 			`Branch: ${session.branch}`,
165: 			`Spec: ${session.specPath}`,
166: 		];
167: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
168: 			tooltipLines.push(`URL: ${session.externalUrl}`);
169: 		}
170: 		if (session.tasks.length > 1) {
171: 			tooltipLines.push(`Tasks: ${session.tasks.length}`);
172: 		}
173: 		if (session.pullRequests.length > 0) {
174: 			tooltipLines.push(`PRs: ${session.pullRequests.length}`);
175: 		}
176: 		item.tooltip = tooltipLines.join("\n");
177: 
178: 		item.iconPath = this.getStatusIcon(session.status);
179: 		item.contextValue =
180: 			session.providerId === activeProviderId && !session.isReadOnly
181: 				? `session.${session.status}`
182: 				: "session.readonly";
183: 
184: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
185: 			item.command = {
186: 				command: "vscode.open",
187: 				title: "Open in Browser",
188: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(session.externalUrl)],
189: 			};
190: 		}
191: 
192: 		return item;
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	private getSessionChildren(session: AgentSession): CloudAgentTreeItem[] {
196: 		const children: CloudAgentTreeItem[] = [];
197: 
198: 		for (const task of session.tasks) {
199: 			const taskItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
200: 				`${task.specTaskId}: ${task.title}`,
201: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
202: 				session,
203: 				task
204: 			);
205: 			taskItem.description = task.status;
206: 			taskItem.iconPath = this.getTaskStatusIcon(task.status);
207: 			taskItem.tooltip = [
208: 				`Task: ${task.title}`,
209: 				`Status: ${task.status}`,
210: 				`Priority: ${task.priority}`,
211: 			].join("\n");
212: 			children.push(taskItem);
213: 		}
214: 
215: 		for (const pr of session.pullRequests) {
216: 			const prItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
217: 				`PR: ${pr.branch}`,
218: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
219: 				session
220: 			);
221: 			prItem.description = pr.state ?? "open";
222: 			prItem.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("git-pull-request");
223: 			prItem.tooltip = pr.url;
224: 			prItem.command = {
225: 				command: "vscode.open",
226: 				title: "Open Pull Request",
227: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(pr.url)],
228: 			};
229: 			children.push(prItem);
230: 		}
231: 
232: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
233: 			const linkItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
234: 				"Open in Provider",
235: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
236: 				session
237: 			);
238: 			linkItem.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("link-external");
239: 			linkItem.tooltip = session.externalUrl;
240: 			linkItem.command = {
241: 				command: "vscode.open",
242: 				title: "Open in Browser",
243: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(session.externalUrl)],
244: 			};
245: 			children.push(linkItem);
246: 		}
247: 
248: 		return children;
249: 	}
250: 
251: 	private getStatusIcon(status: string): ThemeIcon {
252: 		switch (status) {
253: 			case "running":
254: 				return new ThemeIcon("sync~spin");
255: 			case "completed":
256: 				return new ThemeIcon("check");
257: 			case "failed":
258: 				return new ThemeIcon("error");
259: 			case "blocked":
260: 				return new ThemeIcon("warning");
261: 			case "cancelled":
262: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-slash");
263: 			default:
264: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-outline");
265: 		}
266: 	}
267: 
268: 	private getTaskStatusIcon(status: string): ThemeIcon {
269: 		switch (status) {
270: 			case "in_progress":
271: 				return new ThemeIcon("sync~spin");
272: 			case "completed":
273: 				return new ThemeIcon("pass");
274: 			case "failed":
275: 				return new ThemeIcon("error");
276: 			case "skipped":
277: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-slash");
278: 			default:
279: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-outline");
280: 		}
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	dispose(): void {
284: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.dispose();
285: 	}
286: }

(End of file - total 286 lines)

/**
 * Cloud Agent Progress Provider
 *
 * Provider-agnostic tree view data provider for the Cloud Agents sidebar.
 * Shows sessions with task details, external links, and status icons.
 *
 * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
 * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
 */

import {
	EventEmitter,
	ThemeIcon,
	TreeItem,
	TreeItemCollapsibleState,
	Uri,
	commands,
	type Event,
	type TreeDataProvider,
} from "vscode";

state.title: src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts

state.output:
<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * RunningAgentsTreeProvider — "Running Agents" tree view (spec 018, T044).
3:  *
4:  * Renders three groups at the root level:
5:  *
6:  *   1. **Active**             — sessions with a non-terminal lifecycle state.
7:  *   2. **Recent**              — sessions in a terminal lifecycle state.
8:  *   3. **Orphaned worktrees**  — worktree handles whose owner session has
9:  *                                been evicted by retention (§2.4 of the
10:  *                                storage contract). Populated by T075.
11:  *
12:  * Each session leaf carries a click command targeting
13:  * `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession` (wired by T038) and a context value
14:  * that enables right-click menu entries (e.g. "Clean up worktree" for
15:  * worktree-backed sessions, T048).
16:  *
17:  * The provider refreshes whenever either the session store's manifest or
18:  * the in-memory registry emits a change event — so tree updates are
19:  * driven from whichever authority mutated first.
20:  *
21:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/tasks.md §T042 / §T044
22:  */
23: 
24: import {
25: 	type Event,
26: 	EventEmitter,
27: 	ThemeIcon,
28: 	TreeItem,
29: 	TreeItemCollapsibleState,
30: 	type TreeDataProvider,
31: } from "vscode";
32: import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
33: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
34: import type {
35: 	AgentChatSession,
36: 	OrphanedWorktreeEntry,
37: 	SessionManifest,
38: } from "../features/agent-chat/types";
39: 
40: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41: // Public shape
42: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43: 
44: export type RunningAgentsTreeItem = TreeItem & {
45: 	/** Present on session leaf nodes; absent on group and orphan nodes. */
46: 	sessionId?: string;
47: 	/** Present on orphan leaves; absent otherwise. */
48: 	orphanId?: string;
49: 	/** Present on orphan leaves so the right-click handler can build a cleanup payload. */
50: 	orphanAbsolutePath?: string;
51: 	/** Present on orphan leaves. */
52: 	orphanBranchName?: string;
53: 	/** Discriminator for getChildren dispatch. */
54: 	nodeKind:
55: 		| "new-session-leaf"
56: 		| "group-active"
57: 		| "group-recent"
58: 		| "group-orphans"
59: 		| "session"
60: 		| "orphan";
61: };
62: 
63: export interface RunningAgentsTreeProviderOptions {
64: 	readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
65: 	readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
66: 	/**
67: 	 * Event fired whenever the store's manifest changes. Injected so tests
68: 	 * can drive refreshes without reaching into `store.onDidChangeManifest`
69: 	 * private implementation details.
70: 	 */
71: 	readonly storeChangeEvent: Event<SessionManifest>;
72: }
73: 
74: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75: // Constants
76: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77: 
78: const OPEN_FOR_SESSION_COMMAND_ID = "gatomia.agentChat.openForSession";
79: const NEW_SESSION_COMMAND_ID = "gatomia.agentChat.newSession";
80: const NEW_SESSION_LABEL = "+ New agent session…";
81: 
82: const GROUP_LABELS = {
83: 	active: "Active",
84: 	recent: "Recent",
85: 	orphans: "Orphaned worktrees",
86: } as const;
87: 
88: const GROUP_CONTEXT_VALUES = {
89: 	active: "agent-chat-group-active",
90: 	recent: "agent-chat-group-recent",
91: 	orphans: "agent-chat-group-orphans",
92: } as const;
93: 
94: const SESSION_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-session";
95: const SESSION_WORKTREE_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-session-worktree";
96: const ORPHAN_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-orphan-worktree";
97: 
98: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99: // Provider
100: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101: 
102: export class RunningAgentsTreeProvider
103: 	implements TreeDataProvider<RunningAgentsTreeItem>
104: {
105: 	static readonly viewId = "gatomia.views.runningAgents";
106: 
107: 	private readonly _onDidChangeTreeData = new EventEmitter<
108: 		RunningAgentsTreeItem | undefined | null | void
109: 	>();
110: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<
111: 		RunningAgentsTreeItem | undefined | null | void
112: 	> = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
113: 
114: 	private readonly disposables: Array<{ dispose(): void }> = [];
115: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
116: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
117: 
118: 	constructor(options: RunningAgentsTreeProviderOptions) {
119: 		this.store = options.store;
120: 		this.registry = options.registry;
121: 
122: 		this.disposables.push(
123: 			options.storeChangeEvent(() => {
124: 				this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
125: 			})
126: 		);
127: 		this.disposables.push(
128: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
129: 				this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
130: 			})
131: 		);
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	dispose(): void {
135: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
136: 			try {
137: 				d.dispose();
138: 			} catch {
139: 				// best-effort
140: 			}
141: 		}
142: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
143: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.dispose();
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	refresh(): void {
147: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	getTreeItem(element: RunningAgentsTreeItem): TreeItem {
151: 		return element;
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	getChildren(
155: 		element?: RunningAgentsTreeItem
156: 	): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
157: 		if (!element) {
158: 			return Promise.resolve([
159: 				this.newSessionLeaf(),
160: 				this.groupItem("active"),
161: 				this.groupItem("recent"),
162: 				this.groupItem("orphans"),
163: 			]);
164: 		}
165: 
166: 		switch (element.nodeKind) {
167: 			case "group-active":
168: 				return this.renderActiveLeaves();
169: 			case "group-recent":
170: 				return this.renderRecentLeaves();
171: 			case "group-orphans":
172: 				return this.renderOrphanLeaves();
173: 			default:
174: 				return Promise.resolve([]);
175: 		}
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	private newSessionLeaf(): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
179: 		const item = new TreeItem(
180: 			NEW_SESSION_LABEL,
181: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
182: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
183: 		item.nodeKind = "new-session-leaf";
184: 		item.contextValue = "agent-chat-new-session";
185: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("add");
186: 		item.command = {
187: 			command: NEW_SESSION_COMMAND_ID,
188: 			title: NEW_SESSION_LABEL,
189: 		};
190: 		item.tooltip = "Start a new agent chat session";
191: 		return item;
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
195: 	// Group + leaf factories
196: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
197: 
198: 	private groupItem(
199: 		kind: "active" | "recent" | "orphans"
200: 	): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
201: 		const item = new TreeItem(
202: 			GROUP_LABELS[kind],
203: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.Expanded
204: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
205: 		item.contextValue = GROUP_CONTEXT_VALUES[kind];
206: 		item.nodeKind = `group-${kind}` as RunningAgentsTreeItem["nodeKind"];
207: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon(groupIcon(kind));
208: 		return item;
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	private async renderActiveLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
212: 		const sessions = await this.store.listActive();
213: 		return sessions
214: 			.slice()
215: 			.sort(sortByUpdatedAtDesc)
216: 			.map((s) => this.sessionLeaf(s));
217: 	}
218: 
219: 	private async renderRecentLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
220: 		const sessions = await this.store.listRecent();
221: 		return sessions
222: 			.slice()
223: 			.sort(sortByUpdatedAtDesc)
224: 			.map((s) => this.sessionLeaf(s));
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	private async renderOrphanLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
228: 		const orphans = await this.store.listOrphanedWorktrees();
229: 		return orphans
230: 			.slice()
231: 			.sort((a, b) => b.recordedAt - a.recordedAt)
232: 			.map((o) => this.orphanLeaf(o));
233: 	}
234: 
235: 	private sessionLeaf(session: AgentChatSession): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
236: 		const item = new TreeItem(
237: 			session.agentDisplayName,
238: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
239: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
240: 		item.sessionId = session.id;
241: 		item.nodeKind = "session";
242: 		item.description = describeSession(session);
243: 		item.tooltip = `${session.agentDisplayName}${
244: 			session.selectedModeId ? ` · ${session.selectedModeId}` : ""
245: 		} · ${session.executionTarget.kind} · ${session.lifecycleState}`;
246: 		item.contextValue = isWorktreeBacked(session)
247: 			? SESSION_WORKTREE_CONTEXT_VALUE
248: 			: SESSION_CONTEXT_VALUE;
249: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon(lifecycleIcon(session.lifecycleState));
250: 		item.command = {
251: 			command: OPEN_FOR_SESSION_COMMAND_ID,
252: 			title: "Open Agent Chat",
253: 			arguments: [session.id],
254: 		};
255: 		return item;
256: 	}
257: 
258: 	private orphanLeaf(entry: OrphanedWorktreeEntry): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
259: 		const item = new TreeItem(
260: 			entry.branchName,
261: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
262: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
263: 		item.orphanId = entry.sessionId;
264: 		item.orphanAbsolutePath = entry.absolutePath;
265: 		item.orphanBranchName = entry.branchName;
266: 		item.nodeKind = "orphan";
267: 		item.description = entry.absolutePath;
268: 		item.tooltip = `Orphaned worktree at ${entry.absolutePath} (branch ${entry.branchName})`;
269: 		item.contextValue = ORPHAN_CONTEXT_VALUE;
270: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("git-commit");
271: 		return item;
272: 	}
273: }
274: 
275: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
276: // Pure helpers
277: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
278: 
279: function sortByUpdatedAtDesc(a: AgentChatSession, b: AgentChatSession): number {
280: 	return b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt;
281: }
282: 
283: function isWorktreeBacked(session: AgentChatSession): boolean {
284: 	return session.executionTarget.kind === "worktree";
285: }
286: 
287: function describeSession(session: AgentChatSession): string {
288: 	const parts: string[] = [];
289: 	if (session.selectedModeId) {
290: 		parts.push(session.selectedModeId);
291: 	}
292: 	parts.push(session.executionTarget.kind);
293: 	parts.push(session.lifecycleState);
294: 	// T071 — surface the "blocked" flag for sessions waiting on the user so
295: 	// they don't get lost among Active entries.
296: 	if (session.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input") {
297: 		parts.push("(blocked)");
298: 	}
299: 	return parts.join(" · ");
300: }
301: 
302: function groupIcon(kind: "active" | "recent" | "orphans"): string {
303: 	if (kind === "active") {
304: 		return "play-circle";
305: 	}
306: 	if (kind === "recent") {
307: 		return "history";
308: 	}
309: 	return "warning";
310: }
311: 
312: function lifecycleIcon(state: AgentChatSession["lifecycleState"]): string {
313: 	switch (state) {
314: 		case "initializing":
315: 			return "loading~spin";
316: 		case "running":
317: 			return "play";
318: 		case "waiting-for-input":
319: 			return "bell";
320: 		case "completed":
321: 			return "check";
322: 		case "failed":
323: 			return "error";
324: 		case "cancelled":
325: 			return "stop-circle";
326: 		case "ended-by-shutdown":
327: 			return "debug-disconnect";
328: 		default:
329: 			return "circle-outline";
330: 	}
331: }

(End of file - total 331 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * AgentChatPanel — extension-side webview wrapper for one agent chat session.
3:  *
4:  * Responsibilities (Phase 3 / US1 MVP):
5:  *   - Manage a single `vscode.WebviewPanel` instance per session (FR-008).
6:  *   - Load the `agent-chat` webview entry via the shared
7:  *     `getWebviewContent()` helper (registered as a SupportedPage in T004).
8:  *   - Handle the `agent-chat/*` protocol messages relevant to User Story 1:
9:  *     hydration (`ready` → `session/loaded`), input submission with
10:  *     `deliveryStatus` lifecycle, cancel / retry routing.
11:  *   - Forward `AgentChatSessionStore` mutations (manifest + transcript) and
12:  *     registry events to the webview as `messages/appended`,
13:  *     `messages/updated`, `session/lifecycle-changed`.
14:  *
15:  * Out of scope for US1 (deferred to later phases):
16:  *   - worktree banner + cleanup flow (T066, Phase 5)
17:  *   - mode / model / target selectors (T066, Phase 5)
18:  *   - transcript-archive paginated requests (can land later)
19:  *
20:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §4
21:  */
22: 
23: import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
24: import {
25: 	type Disposable,
26: 	EventEmitter,
27: 	type ExtensionContext,
28: 	ViewColumn,
29: 	type WebviewPanel,
30: 	window,
31: } from "vscode";
32: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
33: import type {
34: 	AgentChatPanelLike,
35: 	AgentChatRegistry,
36: } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
37: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
38: import {
39: 	AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS,
40: 	logTelemetry,
41: } from "../features/agent-chat/telemetry";
42: import {
43: 	type AgentChatSession,
44: 	type ChatMessage,
45: 	type SessionLifecycleState,
46: 	TERMINAL_STATES,
47: 	transcriptKeyFor,
48: 	type UserChatMessage,
49: } from "../features/agent-chat/types";
50: 
51: // ============================================================================
52: // Host abstraction
53: // ============================================================================
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Host hook for panel creation. Production wires this to
57:  * `vscode.window.createWebviewPanel`; tests inject a fake so they don't need a
58:  * real VS Code environment.
59:  */
60: export interface AgentChatPanelHost {
61: 	/**
62: 	 * Create the underlying webview panel. The factory returns a minimal
63: 	 * {@link AgentChatPanelLike} plus a `webview` handle the implementation can
64: 	 * post messages to and listen for incoming messages.
65: 	 */
66: 	createPanel(options: { session: AgentChatSession }): HostedPanel;
67: }
68: 
69: /** Minimal webview surface the panel talks to. */
70: export interface HostedWebview {
71: 	postMessage(message: unknown): Thenable<boolean>;
72: 	onDidReceiveMessage(listener: (message: unknown) => void): {
73: 		dispose: () => void;
74: 	};
75: }
76: 
77: /**
78:  * A panel plus its attached webview. Structural so tests can provide a fake
79:  * without importing `vscode.WebviewPanel`.
80:  */
81: export interface HostedPanel extends AgentChatPanelLike {
82: 	webview: HostedWebview;
83: }
84: 
85: /**
86:  * Default host implementation that uses VS Code's real `createWebviewPanel`.
87:  * Exposed so the extension entry point can wire it up in one place.
88:  */
89: export function createDefaultAgentChatPanelHost(
90: 	context: ExtensionContext
91: ): AgentChatPanelHost {
92: 	return {
93: 		createPanel: ({ session }): HostedPanel => {
94: 			const panel: WebviewPanel = window.createWebviewPanel(
95: 				AgentChatPanel.viewType,
96: 				`Agent Chat — ${session.agentDisplayName}`,
97: 				ViewColumn.Beside,
98: 				{
99: 					enableScripts: true,
100: 					retainContextWhenHidden: true,
101: 					localResourceRoots: [context.extensionUri],
102: 				}
103: 			);
104: 			panel.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
105: 				panel.webview,
106: 				context.extensionUri,
107: 				"agent-chat",
108: 				{ "session-id": session.id }
109: 			);
110: 			return wrapVscodePanel(panel);
111: 		},
112: 	};
113: }
114: 
115: /** Adapt a real `vscode.WebviewPanel` to the minimal `HostedPanel` surface. */
116: function wrapVscodePanel(panel: WebviewPanel): HostedPanel {
117: 	const webview: HostedWebview = {
118: 		postMessage: (message: unknown) => panel.webview.postMessage(message),
119: 		onDidReceiveMessage: (
120: 			listener: (message: unknown) => void
121: 		): { dispose: () => void } => {
122: 			const sub = panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage(listener);
123: 			return { dispose: () => sub.dispose() };
124: 		},
125: 	};
126: 	return {
127: 		viewType: panel.viewType,
128: 		webview,
129: 		dispose: () => panel.dispose(),
130: 		reveal: (...args: unknown[]) => {
131: 			const col = args[0] as ViewColumn | undefined;
132: 			panel.reveal(col);
133: 		},
134: 		onDidDispose: (cb: () => void) => {
135: 			const sub = panel.onDidDispose(cb);
136: 			return { dispose: () => sub.dispose() };
137: 		},
138: 	};
139: }
140: 
141: // ============================================================================
142: // Panel options
143: // ============================================================================
144: 
145: export interface AgentChatPanelOptions {
146: 	session: AgentChatSession;
147: 	store: AgentChatSessionStore;
148: 	registry: AgentChatRegistry;
149: 	host: AgentChatPanelHost;
150: }
151: 
152: // ============================================================================
153: // Panel implementation
154: // ============================================================================
155: 
156: export class AgentChatPanel {
157: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.agentChatPanel";
158: 
159: 	private readonly session: AgentChatSession;
160: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
161: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
162: 	private readonly host: AgentChatPanelHost;
163: 
164: 	private panel: HostedPanel | undefined;
165: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
166: 	private opened = false;
167: 	private lastLifecycleState: SessionLifecycleState;
168: 
169: 	/**
170: 	 * Fired exactly once when this `AgentChatPanel` is disposed. Used by
171: 	 * the host wrapper exposed to the registry so the registry can clean
172: 	 * up its `panelsBySessionId` entry without coupling to the inner
173: 	 * `WebviewPanel`.
174: 	 */
175: 	private readonly _onDidDispose = new EventEmitter<void>();
176: 	readonly onDidDispose = this._onDidDispose.event;
177: 
178: 	constructor(options: AgentChatPanelOptions) {
179: 		this.session = options.session;
180: 		this.store = options.store;
181: 		this.registry = options.registry;
182: 		this.host = options.host;
183: 		this.lastLifecycleState = options.session.lifecycleState;
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
187: 	// Lifecycle
188: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
189: 
190: 	/**
191: 	 * Create (or reveal) the webview panel, wire message plumbing, and
192: 	 * subscribe to store/registry updates. Idempotent — subsequent calls
193: 	 * focus the existing panel.
194: 	 */
195: 	open(): void {
196: 		if (this.opened && this.panel) {
197: 			this.panel.reveal(ViewColumn.Beside);
198: 			return;
199: 		}
200: 		this.panel = this.host.createPanel({ session: this.session });
201: 		this.opened = true;
202: 
203: 		// NOTE: We intentionally do NOT call `this.registry.attachPanel(...)`
204: 		// here. The owning command handler (`handleStartNew` /
205: 		// `handleOpenForSession`) is the single source of truth for panel
206: 		// registration — it attaches a wrapper that exposes our
207: 		// `onDidDispose` event so the registry can clean up. Calling
208: 		// `attachPanel` again from this site would violate the registry's
209: 		// "one panel per session" invariant and cause initialization to
210: 		// fail (regression seen in spec 018: "session ... already has a
211: 		// panel" thrown right after the user submitted the new-session
212: 		// prompt).
213: 
214: 		this.disposables.push(
215: 			this.panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((msg) => {
216: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(msg).catch(noop);
217: 			})
218: 		);
219: 
220: 		this.disposables.push(
221: 			this.panel.onDidDispose(() => {
222: 				this.dispose();
223: 			})
224: 		);
225: 
226: 		// Forward manifest changes (lifecycle transitions, session updates).
227: 		this.disposables.push(
228: 			this.store.onDidChangeManifest(() => {
229: 				this.onManifestChanged().catch(noop);
230: 			})
231: 		);
232: 
233: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
234: 			sessionId: this.session.id,
235: 			agentId: this.session.agentId,
236: 		});
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	/**
240: 	 * Forward a transcript mutation (appendMessages / updateMessages) from
241: 	 * external callers. Internal callers never go through this method — they
242: 	 * mutate the store directly and the onDidChangeManifest handler fans out
243: 	 * to the webview.
244: 	 */
245: 	async notifyMessagesAppended(messages: ChatMessage[]): Promise<void> {
246: 		if (!this.panel || messages.length === 0) {
247: 			return;
248: 		}
249: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
250: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/appended",
251: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, messages },
252: 		});
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	async notifyMessagesUpdated(
256: 		updates: Array<{ id: string; patch: Partial<ChatMessage> }>
257: 	): Promise<void> {
258: 		if (!this.panel || updates.length === 0) {
259: 			return;
260: 		}
261: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
262: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/updated",
263: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, updates },
264: 		});
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	dispose(): void {
268: 		if (!this.opened) {
269: 			return;
270: 		}
271: 		this.opened = false;
272: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
273: 			try {
274: 				d.dispose();
275: 			} catch {
276: 				// best-effort
277: 			}
278: 		}
279: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
280: 		if (this.panel) {
281: 			try {
282: 				this.panel.dispose();
283: 			} catch {
284: 				// best-effort
285: 			}
286: 			this.panel = undefined;
287: 		}
288: 		try {
289: 			this._onDidDispose.fire();
290: 		} catch {
291: 			// best-effort: never let listener errors mask a disposal.
292: 		}
293: 		this._onDidDispose.dispose();
294: 	}
295: 
296: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
297: 	// Incoming protocol messages
298: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
299: 
300: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
301: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
302: 		if (!message.type) {
303: 			return;
304: 		}
305: 		switch (message.type) {
306: 			case "agent-chat/ready":
307: 				await this.sendSessionLoaded();
308: 				break;
309: 			case "agent-chat/input/submit":
310: 				await this.handleInputSubmit(
311: 					message.payload as {
312: 						sessionId: string;
313: 						content: string;
314: 						clientMessageId?: string;
315: 					}
316: 				);
317: 				break;
318: 			case "agent-chat/control/cancel":
319: 				await this.routeToRunner("cancel");
320: 				break;
321: 			case "agent-chat/control/retry":
322: 				await this.routeToRunner("retry");
323: 				break;
324: 			default:
325: 				// Unknown / deferred message types log but never crash.
326: 				break;
327: 		}
328: 	}
329: 
330: 	private async sendSessionLoaded(): Promise<void> {
331: 		if (!this.panel) {
332: 			return;
333: 		}
334: 		const current =
335: 			(await this.store.getSession(this.session.id)) ?? this.session;
336: 		const transcript = this.readTranscript(current.id);
337: 		const isReadOnly = current.source === "cloud";
338: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
339: 			type: "agent-chat/session/loaded",
340: 			payload: {
341: 				session: {
342: 					id: current.id,
343: 					source: current.source,
344: 					agentDisplayName: current.agentDisplayName,
345: 					selectedModeId: current.selectedModeId,
346: 					selectedModelId: current.selectedModelId,
347: 					executionTarget: {
348: 						kind: current.executionTarget.kind,
349: 						label: executionTargetLabel(current.executionTarget.kind),
350: 					},
351: 					lifecycleState: current.lifecycleState,
352: 					acceptsFollowUp: computeAcceptsFollowUp(current),
353: 					isReadOnly,
354: 					worktree: current.worktree
355: 						? {
356: 								path: current.worktree.absolutePath,
357: 								branch: current.worktree.branchName,
358: 								status: current.worktree.status,
359: 							}
360: 						: undefined,
361: 					cloud: current.cloud
362: 						? {
363: 								providerId: current.cloud.providerId,
364: 								providerDisplayName: current.cloud.providerId,
365: 								externalUrl: current.cloud.externalUrl,
366: 							}
367: 						: undefined,
368: 				},
369: 				messages: transcript,
370: 				availableModes: [],
371: 				availableModels: [],
372: 				availableTargets: [
373: 					{ kind: "local", label: "Local", enabled: true },
374: 					{ kind: "worktree", label: "Worktree", enabled: true },
375: 					{ kind: "cloud", label: "Cloud", enabled: true },
376: 				],
377: 				hasArchivedTranscript: false,
378: 			},
379: 		});
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	private async handleInputSubmit(payload: {
383: 		sessionId: string;
384: 		content: string;
385: 		clientMessageId?: string;
386: 	}): Promise<void> {
387: 		const current =
388: 			(await this.store.getSession(this.session.id)) ?? this.session;
389: 		const userMessageId = payload.clientMessageId ?? randomUUID();
390: 
391: 		// Optimistic render: append a pending user message immediately.
392: 		const pendingMessage: UserChatMessage = {
393: 			id: userMessageId,
394: 			sessionId: this.session.id,
395: 			timestamp: Date.now(),
396: 			sequence: 0, // store assigns canonical sequence on read; UI just needs ordering
397: 			role: "user",
398: 			content: payload.content,
399: 			isInitialPrompt: false,
400: 			deliveryStatus: "pending",
401: 		};
402: 
403: 		// For cloud (read-only) sessions, immediately reject.
404: 		if (current.source === "cloud") {
405: 			await this.emitUserMessageWithStatus(pendingMessage, {
406: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
407: 				rejectionReason:
408: 					"This is a read-only cloud session. Follow-up input is not supported in v1.",
409: 			});
410: 			return;
411: 		}
412: 
413: 		// Terminal sessions cannot accept more input.
414: 		if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(current.lifecycleState)) {
415: 			await this.emitUserMessageWithStatus(pendingMessage, {
416: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
417: 				rejectionReason: `Session is in terminal state (${current.lifecycleState}); cannot accept follow-up input.`,
418: 			});
419: 			return;
420: 		}
421: 
422: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.session.id);
423: 		if (!runner) {
424: 			await this.emitUserMessageWithStatus(pendingMessage, {
425: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
426: 				rejectionReason: "No active runner attached to this session.",
427: 			});
428: 			return;
429: 		}
430: 
431: 		// Emit the pending message up-front.
432: 		await this.sendMessagesAppended([pendingMessage]);
433: 
434: 		if (!runner.submit) {
435: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
436: 				{
437: 					id: pendingMessage.id,
438: 					patch: {
439: 						deliveryStatus: "rejected",
440: 						rejectionReason: "This runner does not accept follow-up input.",
441: 					} as Partial<ChatMessage>,
442: 				},
443: 			]);
444: 			return;
445: 		}
446: 		try {
447: 			await runner.submit(payload.content);
448: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
449: 				{
450: 					id: pendingMessage.id,
451: 					patch: { deliveryStatus: "delivered" } as Partial<ChatMessage>,
452: 				},
453: 			]);
454: 		} catch (err) {
455: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
456: 				{
457: 					id: pendingMessage.id,
458: 					patch: {
459: 						deliveryStatus: "rejected",
460: 						rejectionReason: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
461: 					} as Partial<ChatMessage>,
462: 				},
463: 			]);
464: 		}
465: 	}
466: 
467: 	private async routeToRunner(method: "cancel" | "retry"): Promise<void> {
468: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.session.id);
469: 		if (!runner) {
470: 			return;
471: 		}
472: 		try {
473: 			if (method === "cancel") {
474: 				await runner.cancel();
475: 			} else if (runner.retry) {
476: 				await runner.retry();
477: 			}
478: 		} catch (err) {
479: 			logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.ERROR, {
480: 				sessionId: this.session.id,
481: 				stage: method,
482: 				error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
483: 			});
484: 		}
485: 	}
486: 
487: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
488: 	// Outgoing protocol messages
489: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
490: 
491: 	private async emitUserMessageWithStatus(
492: 		base: UserChatMessage,
493: 		patch: Partial<UserChatMessage>
494: 	): Promise<void> {
495: 		await this.sendMessagesAppended([base]);
496: 		await this.sendMessagesUpdated([{ id: base.id, patch }]);
497: 	}
498: 
499: 	private async sendMessagesAppended(messages: ChatMessage[]): Promise<void> {
500: 		if (!this.panel) {
501: 			return;
502: 		}
503: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
504: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/appended",
505: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, messages },
506: 		});
507: 	}
508: 
509: 	private async sendMessagesUpdated(
510: 		updates: Array<{ id: string; patch: Partial<ChatMessage> }>
511: 	): Promise<void> {
512: 		if (!this.panel) {
513: 			return;
514: 		}
515: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
516: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/updated",
517: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, updates },
518: 		});
519: 	}
520: 
521: 	private async onManifestChanged(): Promise<void> {
522: 		if (!this.panel) {
523: 			return;
524: 		}
525: 		const current = await this.store.getSession(this.session.id);
526: 		if (!current) {
527: 			return;
528: 		}
529: 		if (current.lifecycleState !== this.lastLifecycleState) {
530: 			const from = this.lastLifecycleState;
531: 			this.lastLifecycleState = current.lifecycleState;
532: 			await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
533: 				type: "agent-chat/session/lifecycle-changed",
534: 				payload: {
535: 					sessionId: this.session.id,
536: 					from,
537: 					to: current.lifecycleState,
538: 					at: Date.now(),
539: 				},
540: 			});
541: 		}
542: 		// Transcript changes are surfaced via messages/appended when the runner
543: 		// calls store.appendMessages. The panel re-reads the transcript from
544: 		// memento and diffs in a minimal way.
545: 		await this.flushTranscriptDeltas();
546: 	}
547: 
548: 	private readonly knownMessageIds = new Set<string>();
549: 
550: 	private async flushTranscriptDeltas(): Promise<void> {
551: 		if (!this.panel) {
552: 			return;
553: 		}
554: 		const transcript = this.readTranscript(this.session.id);
555: 		const fresh: ChatMessage[] = [];
556: 		for (const msg of transcript) {
557: 			if (!this.knownMessageIds.has(msg.id)) {
558: 				this.knownMessageIds.add(msg.id);
559: 				fresh.push(msg);
560: 			}
561: 		}
562: 		if (fresh.length > 0) {
563: 			await this.sendMessagesAppended(fresh);
564: 		}
565: 	}
566: 
567: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
568: 	// Helpers
569: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
570: 
571: 	private readTranscript(sessionId: string): ChatMessage[] {
572: 		// Reach into the store's backing memento keys. The store owns its
573: 		// schema; this helper is a tightly-scoped read path that avoids
574: 		// introducing a public API just for this case.
575: 		const key = transcriptKeyFor(sessionId);
576: 		const mem = (
577: 			this.store as unknown as {
578: 				workspaceState: {
579: 					get<T>(k: string): T | undefined;
580: 				};
581: 			}
582: 		).workspaceState;
583: 		const raw = mem.get<{ messages: ChatMessage[] }>(key);
584: 		return raw?.messages ?? [];
585: 	}
586: }
587: 
588: // ============================================================================
589: // Small helpers
590: // ============================================================================
591: 
592: function executionTargetLabel(kind: "local" | "worktree" | "cloud"): string {
593: 	switch (kind) {
594: 		case "local":
595: 			return "Local";
596: 		case "worktree":
597: 			return "Worktree";
598: 		case "cloud":
599: 			return "Cloud";
600: 		default:
601: 			return "Local";
602: 	}
603: }
604: 
605: function noop(): void {
606: 	// Intentionally empty — used as a silent .catch handler.
607: }
608: 
609: function computeAcceptsFollowUp(session: AgentChatSession): boolean {
610: 	if (session.source === "cloud") {
611: 		return false;
612: 	}
613: 	if (session.capabilities.source === "none") {
614: 		return true;
615: 	}
616: 	return session.capabilities.acceptsFollowUp;
617: }

(End of file - total 617 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
/**
 * AgentChatPanel — extension-side webview wrapper for one agent chat session.
 *
 * Responsibilities (Phase 3 / US1 MVP):
 *   - Manage a single `vscode.WebviewPanel` instance per session (FR-008).
 *   - Load the `agent-chat` webview entry via the shared
 *     `getWebviewContent()` helper (registered as a SupportedPage in T004).
 *   - Handle the `agent-chat/*` protocol messages relevant to User Story 1:
 *     hydration (`ready` → `session/loaded`), input submission with
 *     `deliveryStatus` lifecycle, cancel / retry routing.
 *   - Forward `AgentChatSessionStore` mutations (manifest + transcript) and
 *     registry events to the webview as `messages/appended`,
 *     `messages/updated`, `session/lifecycle-changed`.
 *
 * Out of scope for US1 (deferred to later phases):
 *   - worktree banner + cleanup flow (T066, Phase 5)
 *   - mode / model / target selectors (T066, Phase 5)
 *   - transcript-archive paginated requests (can land later)
 *
 * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §4

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Progress Panel
3:  *
4:  * Provider-agnostic webview panel for rich session progress UI.
5:  * Replaces the Devin-specific progress panel.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
9:  */
10: 
11: import {
12: 	type Disposable,
13: 	type ExtensionContext,
14: 	ViewColumn,
15: 	type WebviewPanel,
16: 	window,
17: } from "vscode";
18: import type { AgentSessionStorage } from "../features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage";
19: import { logInfo } from "../features/cloud-agents/logging";
20: import type { ProviderRegistry } from "../features/cloud-agents/provider-registry";
21: import type { AgentSession } from "../features/cloud-agents/types";
22: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
23: 
24: // ============================================================================
25: // CloudAgentProgressPanel
26: // ============================================================================
27: 
28: /**
29:  * Webview panel for Cloud Agent session progress display.
30:  * Renders sessions via the shared webview React app and
31:  * communicates through the VS Code postMessage bridge.
32:  *
33:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
34:  */
35: export class CloudAgentProgressPanel {
36: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.cloudAgentProgress";
37: 	private panel: WebviewPanel | undefined;
38: 	private disposables: Disposable[] = [];
39: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
40: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
41: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage;
42: 
43: 	constructor(
44: 		context: ExtensionContext,
45: 		registry: ProviderRegistry,
46: 		sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage
47: 	) {
48: 		this.context = context;
49: 		this.registry = registry;
50: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	/**
54: 	 * Show or create the webview panel.
55: 	 */
56: 	show(): void {
57: 		if (this.panel) {
58: 			this.panel.reveal(ViewColumn.Beside);
59: 			this.sendSessionData();
60: 			return;
61: 		}
62: 
63: 		this.panel = window.createWebviewPanel(
64: 			CloudAgentProgressPanel.viewType,
65: 			"Cloud Agent Progress",
66: 			ViewColumn.Beside,
67: 			{
68: 				enableScripts: true,
69: 				retainContextWhenHidden: true,
70: 				localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
71: 			}
72: 		);
73: 
74: 		this.panel.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
75: 			this.panel.webview,
76: 			this.context.extensionUri,
77: 			"cloud-agent-progress"
78: 		);
79: 
80: 		this.panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage(
81: 			(message: { type: string; payload?: Record<string, unknown> }) => {
82: 				this.handleMessage(message);
83: 			},
84: 			undefined,
85: 			this.disposables
86: 		);
87: 
88: 		this.panel.onDidDispose(
89: 			() => {
90: 				this.panel = undefined;
91: 				for (const d of this.disposables) {
92: 					d.dispose();
93: 				}
94: 				this.disposables = [];
95: 			},
96: 			undefined,
97: 			this.disposables
98: 		);
99: 
100: 		this.sendSessionData();
101: 		logInfo("Cloud Agent progress panel opened");
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/**
105: 	 * Push fresh session data to the webview.
106: 	 */
107: 	async sendSessionData(): Promise<void> {
108: 		if (!this.panel) {
109: 			return;
110: 		}
111: 
112: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
113: 		const sessions = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
114: 
115: 		const sessionViews = sessions.map((s: AgentSession) => ({
116: 			localId: s.localId,
117: 			providerId: s.providerId,
118: 			status: s.status,
119: 			displayStatus: active ? active.getStatusDisplay(s) : s.status,
120: 			branch: s.branch,
121: 			specPath: s.specPath,
122: 			externalUrl: s.externalUrl,
123: 			createdAt: s.createdAt,
124: 			updatedAt: s.updatedAt,
125: 			isReadOnly: s.isReadOnly,
126: 			tasks: s.tasks.map((t) => ({
127: 				taskId: t.id,
128: 				specTaskId: t.specTaskId,
129: 				title: t.title,
130: 				priority: t.priority,
131: 				status: t.status,
132: 			})),
133: 			pullRequests: s.pullRequests.map((pr) => ({
134: 				url: pr.url,
135: 				state: pr.state ?? "open",
136: 				branch: pr.branch,
137: 			})),
138: 		}));
139: 
140: 		this.panel.webview.postMessage({
141: 			type: "session-update",
142: 			payload: {
143: 				sessions: sessionViews,
144: 				activeProvider: active
145: 					? { id: active.metadata.id, displayName: active.metadata.displayName }
146: 					: null,
147: 			},
148: 		});
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	/**
152: 	 * Dispose the panel.
153: 	 */
154: 	dispose(): void {
155: 		this.panel?.dispose();
156: 		this.panel = undefined;
157: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
158: 			d.dispose();
159: 		}
160: 		this.disposables = [];
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	private handleMessage(message: {
164: 		type: string;
165: 		payload?: Record<string, unknown>;
166: 	}): void {
167: 		switch (message.type) {
168: 			case "refresh-status":
169: 				this.sendSessionData();
170: 				break;
171: 			case "open-external": {
172: 				const url = message.payload?.url;
173: 				if (typeof url === "string") {
174: 					import("vscode").then(({ env, Uri }) => {
175: 						env.openExternal(Uri.parse(url));
176: 					});
177: 				}
178: 				break;
179: 			}
180: 			default:
181: 				break;
182: 		}
183: 	}
184: }

(End of file - total 184 lines)
</content>

I’m checking the current page registrations, UI entry points, and any existing orchestration code so I can slot this in with the smallest possible change.

1: import {
2: 	commands,
3: 	env,

14: 	logTelemetry,

18: 	OrchestrationSessionProjection,
19: } from "../features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model";
20: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
21: 
22: interface OrchestrationViewProviderOptions {
23: 	readonly context: ExtensionContext;
24: 	readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
25: 	readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
26: }
27: 
28: export class OrchestrationViewProvider implements WebviewViewProvider {
29: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.views.orchestration";
30: 
31: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
32: 	private readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
33: 	private readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
34: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
35: 	private view: WebviewView | undefined;
36: 
37: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationViewProviderOptions) {
38: 		this.context = options.context;
39: 		this.readModel = options.readModel;
40: 		this.outputChannel = options.outputChannel;
41: 
42: 		this.disposables.push(
43: 			this.readModel.onDidChange(() => {
44: 				this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
45: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
46: 						`[Orchestration] snapshot push failed: ${formatError(error)}`
47: 					);
48: 				});
49: 			})
50: 		);
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	resolveWebviewView(
54: 		webviewView: WebviewView,
55: 		_context: WebviewViewResolveContext,
56: 		_token: CancellationToken
57: 	): void {
58: 		this.view = webviewView;
59: 
60: 		webviewView.webview.options = {
61: 			enableScripts: true,
62: 			localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
63: 		};
64: 		webviewView.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
65: 			webviewView.webview,
66: 			this.context.extensionUri,
67: 			"orchestration"
68: 		);
69: 
70: 		this.disposables.push(
71: 			webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((message) => {
72: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(message).catch((error) => {
73: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
74: 						`[Orchestration] message handling failed: ${formatError(error)}`
75: 					);
76: 				});
77: 			})
78: 		);
79: 
80: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
81: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
82: 				`[Orchestration] initial snapshot failed: ${formatError(error)}`
83: 			);
84: 		});
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	async reveal(): Promise<void> {
88: 		await commands.executeCommand(
89: 			`${OrchestrationViewProvider.viewType}.focus`
90: 		);
91: 		this.view?.show?.(true);
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	refresh(): void {
95: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
96: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
97: 				`[Orchestration] refresh failed: ${formatError(error)}`
98: 			);
99: 		});
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	dispose(): void {
103: 		for (const disposable of this.disposables) {
104: 			try {
105: 				disposable.dispose();
106: 			} catch {
107: 				// best-effort
108: 			}
109: 		}
110: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
114: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
115: 		if (!message?.type) {
116: 			return;
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		switch (message.type) {
120: 			case "orchestration/ready":
121: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
122: 				return;
123: 			case "orchestration/refresh":
124: 				await commands
125: 					.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents")
126: 					.catch(() => {
127: 						// best-effort
128: 					});
129: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
130: 				return;
131: 			case "orchestration/open-session": {
132: 				const sessionId = (
133: 					message.payload as { sessionId?: string } | undefined
134: 				)?.sessionId;
135: 				if (!sessionId) {
136: 					return;
137: 				}
138: 				const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
139: 				const session = snapshot.sessions.find(
140: 					(entry) => entry.id === sessionId
141: 				);
142: 				if (session) {
143: 					await this.openSession(session);
144: 				}
145: 				return;
146: 			}
147: 			case "orchestration/open-existing-surface": {
148: 				const source = (message.payload as { source?: string } | undefined)
149: 					?.source;
150: 				if (source === "cloud-agent") {
151: 					await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
152: 					return;
153: 				}
154: 				await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.agentChat.openPanel");
155: 				return;
156: 			}
157: 			case "orchestration/open-external": {
158: 				const url = (message.payload as { url?: string } | undefined)?.url;
159: 				if (url) {
160: 					await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(url));
161: 				}
162: 				return;
163: 			}
164: 			default:
165: 				return;
166: 		}
167: 	}
168: 
169: 	private async pushSnapshot(): Promise<void> {
170: 		if (!this.view) {
171: 			return;
172: 		}
173: 		const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
174: 		await this.view.webview.postMessage({
175: 			type: "orchestration/snapshot",
176: 			payload: snapshot,
177: 		});
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	private async openSession(
181: 		session: OrchestrationSessionProjection
182: 	): Promise<void> {
183: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
184: 			sessionId: session.sourceSessionId,
185: 			source: session.source,
186: 			surface: "orchestration",
187: 		});
188: 
189: 		if (session.openSessionCommand.kind === "agent-chat") {
190: 			await commands.executeCommand(
191: 				"gatomia.agentChat.openForSession",
192: 				session.openSessionCommand.sessionId
193: 			);
194: 			return;
195: 		}
196: 
197: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
198: 			await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(session.externalUrl));
199: 			return;
200: 		}
201: 
202: 		await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
203: 	}
204: }
205: 
206: function formatError(error: unknown): string {
207: 	return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
208: }

1: ---
2: name: orchestration-ui-contract
3: description: UI contract for the phase 01 orchestration prototype
4: author: OpenCode
5: version: v0.0.1
6: ---
7: 
8: # Orchestration UI Contract
9: 
10: See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]
11: 
12: ## Inbound Messages
13: 
14: - `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
15: - `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
16: - `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
17: - `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
18: - `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.
19: 
20: ## Outbound Messages
21: 
22: - `orchestration/snapshot`: extension publishes the normalized orchestration snapshot.
23: 
24: ## Snapshot Shape
25: 
26: - `sessions[]`: normalized session cards with `id`, `source`, `sourceSessionId`, `title`, `agentName`, `state`, `bucket`, timestamps, blocking metadata, worktree metadata, and open-session routing hints.
27: - `activeProvider`: current cloud provider metadata when present.
28: - `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp.
29: - `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations.
30: 
31: ## Empty and Degraded States
32: 
33: - Empty state explains that no local or cloud sessions exist yet.
34: - Degraded state explains when cloud storage or provider wiring is unavailable.
35: - Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active list.

(End of file - total 35 lines)
</content>

---
name: orchestration-ui-contract
description: UI contract for the phase 01 orchestration prototype
author: OpenCode
version: v0.0.1
---

# Orchestration UI Contract

See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]

## Inbound Messages

- `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
- `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
- `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
- `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.

## Outbound Messages

21: 
22: interface OrchestrationViewProviderOptions {
23: 	readonly context: ExtensionContext;
24: 	readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
25: 	readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
26: }
27: 
28: export class OrchestrationViewProvider implements WebviewViewProvider {
29: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.views.orchestration";
30: 
31: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
32: 	private readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
33: 	private readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
34: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
35: 	private view: WebviewView | undefined;
36: 
37: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationViewProviderOptions) {
38: 		this.context = options.context;
39: 		this.readModel = options.readModel;
40: 		this.outputChannel = options.outputChannel;
41: 
42: 		this.disposables.push(
43: 			this.readModel.onDidChange(() => {
44: 				this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
45: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
46: 						`[Orchestration] snapshot push failed: ${formatError(error)}`
47: 					);
48: 				});
49: 			})
50: 		);
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	resolveWebviewView(
54: 		webviewView: WebviewView,
55: 		_context: WebviewViewResolveContext,
56: 		_token: CancellationToken
57: 	): void {
58: 		this.view = webviewView;
59: 
60: 		webviewView.webview.options = {
61: 			enableScripts: true,
62: 			localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
63: 		};
64: 		webviewView.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
65: 			webviewView.webview,
66: 			this.context.extensionUri,
67: 			"orchestration"
68: 		);
69: 
70: 		this.disposables.push(
71: 			webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((message) => {
72: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(message).catch((error) => {
73: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
74: 						`[Orchestration] message handling failed: ${formatError(error)}`
75: 					);
76: 				});
77: 			})
78: 		);
79: 
80: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
81: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
82: 				`[Orchestration] initial snapshot failed: ${formatError(error)}`
83: 			);
84: 		});
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	async reveal(): Promise<void> {
88: 		await commands.executeCommand(
89: 			`${OrchestrationViewProvider.viewType}.focus`
90: 		);
91: 		this.view?.show?.(true);
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	refresh(): void {
95: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
96: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
97: 				`[Orchestration] refresh failed: ${formatError(error)}`
98: 			);
99: 		});
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	dispose(): void {
103: 		for (const disposable of this.disposables) {
104: 			try {
105: 				disposable.dispose();
106: 			} catch {
107: 				// best-effort
108: 			}
109: 		}
110: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
114: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
115: 		if (!message?.type) {
116: 			return;
117: 		}
118: 
119: 			switch (message.type) {
120: 				case "orchestration/ready":
121: 					await this.pushSnapshot();
122: 					return;
123: 				case "orchestration/refresh":
124: 					await this.focusTreeView("gatomia.views.cloudAgents").catch(() => {
125: 						// best-effort
126: 					});
127: 					await commands
128: 						.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents")
129: 						.catch(() => {
130: 							// best-effort
131: 						});
132: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
133: 				return;
134: 			case "orchestration/open-session": {
135: 				const sessionId = (
136: 					message.payload as { sessionId?: string } | undefined
137: 				)?.sessionId;
138: 				if (!sessionId) {
139: 					return;
140: 				}
141: 				const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
142: 				const session = snapshot.sessions.find(
143: 					(entry) => entry.id === sessionId
144: 				);
145: 				if (session) {
146: 					await this.openSession(session);
147: 				}
148: 				return;
149: 			}
150: 				case "orchestration/open-existing-surface": {
151: 					const source = (message.payload as { source?: string } | undefined)
152: 						?.source;
153: 					if (source === "cloud-agent") {
154: 						await this.focusTreeView("gatomia.views.cloudAgents").catch(() => {
155: 							// best-effort
156: 						});
157: 						await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
158: 						return;
159: 					}
160: 					await this.focusTreeView("gatomia.views.runningAgents").catch(() => {
161: 						// best-effort
162: 					});
163: 					await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.agentChat.openPanel");
164: 					return;
165: 				}
166: 			case "orchestration/open-external": {
167: 				const url = (message.payload as { url?: string } | undefined)?.url;
168: 				if (url) {
169: 					await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(url));
170: 				}
171: 				return;
172: 			}
173: 			default:
174: 				return;
175: 		}
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	private async pushSnapshot(): Promise<void> {
179: 		if (!this.view) {
180: 			return;
181: 		}
182: 		const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
183: 		await this.view.webview.postMessage({
184: 			type: "orchestration/snapshot",
185: 			payload: snapshot,
186: 		});
187: 	}
188:

190: 		session: OrchestrationSessionProjection
191: 	): Promise<void> {

195: 			surface: "orchestration",
196: 		});
197: 
198: 		if (session.openSessionCommand.kind === "agent-chat") {

209: 		}
210: 
211: 		await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
212: 	}
213:

217: }

-          "text": ""# Context

Your name is **Dori**, a Maestro-managed AI agent. You are executing tasks from a **Playbook** — a collection of Auto Run documents. Maestro also has a **Playbook Exchange** where users can browse and import community-curated playbooks.

- **Agent Path:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode

- **Auto Run Folder:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs

- **Working Folder for Temporary Files:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Working

If you need to create the working folder, do so.

---

## CRITICAL: Response Format Requirement

**Your response MUST begin with a specific, actionable synopsis of what you accomplished.**

- GOOD examples: \"Added pagination to the user list component\", \"Fixed authentication timeout bug in login.ts\", \"Refactored database queries to use prepared statements\"
- BAD examples: \"The task is complete\", \"Task completed successfully\", \"Done\", \"Finished the task\"

The synopsis is displayed in the History panel and must describe the actual work done, not just that work was done.

---

## Structured Output Artifacts

When creating documentation, research notes, reports, or any knowledge artifacts (not source code), use **structured Markdown** by default:

### YAML Front Matter

```yaml
---
type: research | note | report | analysis | reference
title: Descriptive Title
created: YYYY-MM-DD
tags:
  - relevant-tag
related:
  - '[[Other-Document]]'
---
```

### Wiki-Link Cross-References

Use `[[Document-Name]]` syntax to connect related documents. This enables graph exploration in Maestro's DocGraph viewer and tools like Obsidian.

### Folder Organization

Organize artifacts in logical folders by entity type or domain:

```
docs/
├── research/
│   ├── topic-a.md
│   └── topic-b.md
├── architecture/
│   └── system-design.md
└── decisions/
    └── adr-001-choice.md
```

**When to apply:** Research findings, competitive analysis, architecture decisions, technical specs, meeting notes, reference docs, glossaries.

**When NOT to apply:** Source code files, config files (JSON/YAML), generated assets, temporary files.

## Instructions

1. Project Orientation
   Begin by reviewing CLAUDE.md (when available) in this folder to understand the project's structure, conventions, and workflow expectations.

2. Task Selection
   Process the FIRST unchecked task (- [ ]) from top to bottom. Note that there may be relevant images associated with the task. If there are, analyze them, and include in your final synopsis back how many images you analyzed in preparation for solving the task.

   IMPORTANT: You will only work on this single task. If the task description contains multiple steps or subtasks (e.g., bullet points, numbered lists, or semicolon-separated items), treat them as one cohesive unit—they were intentionally grouped by the document author to share context. Complete all parts before marking done. Do not move on to the next major checkbox item.

3. Task Evaluation
   - Fully understand the task and inspect the relevant code.
   - Identify all subtasks within the current checkbox item.
   - There will be future runs to take care of other checkbox items.

4. Task Implementation
   - **Before creating new code**, search for existing implementations, utilities, helpers, or patterns in the codebase that can be reused or extended. Avoid duplicating functionality that already exists.
   - Implement the task according to the project's established style, architecture, and coding norms.
   - Ensure that test cases are created, and that they pass.
   - Ensure you haven't broken any existing test cases.

5. Completion + Reporting
   - Mark the task as completed by changing \"- [ ]\" to \"- [x]\".
   - Begin your response with the specific synopsis (see \"Response Format Requirement\" above).
   - Follow with any relevant details about:
     - Implementation approach or key decisions made
     - Why the task was intentionally skipped (if applicable)
     - If implementation failed, explain the failure and do NOT check off the item.

6. Version Control
   For any code or documentation changes, if we're in a Github repo:
   - Commit using a descriptive message prefixed with \"MAESTRO: \".
   - Push to GitHub.
   - Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, or any other top-level documentation if appropriate.

7. Exit Immediately
   After completing (or skipping) your task, EXIT. Do not proceed to additional tasks—another agent instance will handle them. If there are no remaining open tasks, exit immediately and state that there is nothing left to do.

<content>
1: # Phase 01: Running Agents Orchestration Prototype
2: 
3: This phase delivers the first end-to-end orchestration prototype for GatomIA by turning the existing running-agent, agent-chat, and cloud-agent surfaces into a single coherent experience that visibly tracks active sessions. It matters because it gives Product Engineers and Software Engineers a working orchestration dashboard immediately, proves the new direction with minimal refactoring risk, and creates the foundation that later phases will extend with hooks, visual flows, and autonomous task execution.
4: 
5: - NEVER push de code
6: 
7: ## Tasks
8: 
9: - [x] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
10:   - Inspect and map the current behavior in `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`, `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`, `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`, and `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
11:   - Identify the smallest viable prototype that can be built by composing existing session state, registry events, and view registration instead of introducing a second orchestration stack
12:   - Capture the target slice in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md` using Obsidian-style markdown with YAML front matter, links to `[[Running-Agents-Model]]` and `[[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]`, and explicit notes about reused modules versus new modules
13:   - Note: baseline mapping and reuse-first target slice captured on 2026-04-30 in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md`
14: 
15: - [x] Implement a unified orchestration read model for active and recent agents:
16:   - Reuse existing session and provider contracts before creating new types; search for agent session models, lifecycle state helpers, and existing telemetry patterns first
17:   - Create or refactor a narrow service that merges local running-agent sessions and cloud-agent session progress into one normalized projection for the UI
18:   - Preserve current tree-view behavior while exposing the same normalized data to a webview-friendly bridge so the prototype has one source of truth
19:   - Include lifecycle metadata that is already available or derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status
20:   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by extending `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` to merge persisted and live agent-chat sessions, derive transcript-backed titles/activity, and normalize provider display names for cloud sessions without changing existing tree providers.
21:   - Validation: re-verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.test.ts` and `npm run check`; the check required clearing a disposable oversized `.entire/tmp` session artifact before passing.
22: 
23: - [x] Build the first orchestration prototype view that visibly works inside the extension:
24:   - Reuse the existing page registry, panel, and bridge patterns before introducing new routing or state libraries
25:   - Add a dedicated orchestration view or panel that renders the normalized session projection with clear columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed work
26:   - Ensure the prototype allows at minimum: open session, refresh session state, inspect current status, and navigate back to the existing agent-chat or cloud-agent experience
27:   - Keep the UI intentionally simple but visually compelling so the user can see live orchestration progress as soon as the phase is complete
28:   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by wiring the existing orchestration webview provider into the extension bootstrap, adding a simple four-lane card UI in `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx`, and fixing cloud-session navigation so the prototype can jump back to the Cloud Agents surface instead of only refreshing state.
29:   - Validation: re-verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/webview/cloud-agent-progress-view.test.tsx` and `npm run check`.
30: 
31: - [ ] Connect orchestration events, telemetry, and empty states so the prototype behaves like a real product surface:
32:   - Reuse existing event emitters, output channels, and telemetry helpers before adding new instrumentation paths
33:   - Make tree updates and orchestration view updates respond to session-store, registry, and cloud-provider changes without manual reloads where possible
34:   - Add actionable empty, loading, and degraded states that explain whether there are no sessions, missing providers, or failed status reads
35:   - Write implementation notes in `docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md` with YAML front matter and wiki-links to `[[Running-Agents-Prototype]]`
36: 
37: - [ ] Write test coverage for the orchestration prototype behavior:
38:   - Add or update unit tests for the normalized orchestration read model, lifecycle grouping, and any new state selectors or adapters
39:   - Add webview or provider-focused tests that verify the prototype renders meaningful empty, active, and failed states from representative session data
40:   - Prefer extending existing test suites around providers and stores before creating entirely new harnesses
41: 
42: - [ ] Run the orchestration quality gates and fix failures until the prototype is working end-to-end:
43:   - Run the relevant targeted tests for orchestration changes first
44:   - Run `npm run check`
45:   - Run the full affected test command if targeted tests expose regressions that require broader validation
46:   - Verify the extension can surface the new orchestration prototype without breaking the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents views

(End of file - total 46 lines)
</content>",

## CRITICAL: Response Format Requirement

**Your response MUST begin with a specific, actionable synopsis of what you accomplished.**

- GOOD examples: \"Added pagination to the user list component\", \"Fixed authentication timeout bug in login.ts\", \"Refactored database queries to use prepared statements\"
- BAD examples: \"The task is complete\", \"Task completed successfully\", \"Done\", \"Finished the task\"

The synopsis is displayed in the History panel and must describe the actual work done, not just that work was done.

---

## Structured Output Artifacts

When creating documentation, research notes, reports, or any knowledge artifacts (not source code), use **structured Markdown** by default:

### YAML Front Matter

```yaml
---
type: research | note | report | analysis | reference
title: Descriptive Title
created: YYYY-MM-DD
tags:
  - relevant-tag
related:
  - '[[Other-Document]]'
---
```

### Wiki-Link Cross-References

Use `[[Document-Name]]` syntax to connect related documents. This enables graph exploration in Maestro's DocGraph viewer and tools like Obsidian.

### Folder Organization

Organize artifacts in logical folders by entity type or domain:

```
docs/
├── research/
│   ├── topic-a.md
│   └── topic-b.md
├── architecture/
│   └── system-design.md
└── decisions/
    └── adr-001-choice.md
```

**When to apply:** Research findings, competitive analysis, architecture decisions, technical specs, meeting notes, reference docs, glossaries.

**When NOT to apply:** Source code files, config files (JSON/YAML), generated assets, temporary files.

## Instructions

1. Project Orientation
   Begin by reviewing CLAUDE.md (when available) in this folder to understand the project's structure, conventions, and workflow expectations.

2. Task Selection
   Process the FIRST unchecked task (- [ ]) from top to bottom. Note that there may be relevant images associated with the task. If there are, analyze them, and include in your final synopsis back how many images you analyzed in preparation for solving the task.

   IMPORTANT: You will only work on this single task. If the task description contains multiple steps or subtasks (e.g., bullet points, numbered lists, or semicolon-separated items), treat them as one cohesive unit—they were intentionally grouped by the document author to share context. Complete all parts before marking done. Do not move on to the next major checkbox item.

3. Task Evaluation
   - Fully understand the task and inspect the relevant code.
   - Identify all subtasks within the current checkbox item.
   - There will be future runs to take care of other checkbox items.

4. Task Implementation
   - **Before creating new code**, search for existing implementations, utilities, helpers, or patterns in the codebase that can be reused or extended. Avoid duplicating functionality that already exists.
   - Implement the task according to the project's established style, architecture, and coding norms.
   - Ensure that test cases are created, and that they pass.
   - Ensure you haven't broken any existing test cases.

5. Completion + Reporting
   - Mark the task as completed by changing \"- [ ]\" to \"- [x]\".
   - Begin your response with the specific synopsis (see \"Response Format Requirement\" above).
   - Follow with any relevant details about:
     - Implementation approach or key decisions made
     - Why the task was intentionally skipped (if applicable)
     - If implementation failed, explain the failure and do NOT check off the item.

6. Version Control
   For any code or documentation changes, if we're in a Github repo:
   - Commit using a descriptive message prefixed with \"MAESTRO: \".
   - Push to GitHub.
   - Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, or any other top-level documentation if appropriate.

7. Exit Immediately
   After completing (or skipping) your task, EXIT. Do not proceed to additional tasks—another agent instance will handle them. If there are no remaining open tasks, exit immediately and state that there is nothing left to do.
